pi_items.source: Specified in setup_task.sh and export_result.sh shell scripts
EIA-310 and IEC 60297 standards specify 19-inch (482.6 mm) as the standard width for rack panels. Confirmed via https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/19-inch_rack

EIA-310 standard specifies 1U equipment front panel height as 1.71875 inches = 43.66 mm (0.03125 inches less than the full 1.75 inch rack unit to allow spacing). The metadata value of 43.7 mm is within 0.04 mm of the standard. Confirmed via https://www.racksolutions.com/news/blog/rack-unit-converter-how-to-convert-rack-u-height/ and multiple sources
EIA-310 standard specifies horizontal hole spacing as 18 5/16 inches = 18.3125 inches = 465.0625 mm ≈ 465.1 mm. Confirmed via https://www.audiorax.com/rack-rail-hole-spacing-explained

Metric M6 mounting holes are an accepted standard for rack panels. While 10-32 UNC threads are more common in US installations, metric 6mm holes are a valid EIA-310 alternative. Confirmed via https://www.audiorax.com/rack-rail-hole-spacing-explained and https://www.racksolutions.com/news/blog/rack-screw-holes/

Standard XLR connectors (commonly used for audio, DMX, and power in rack equipment) have an external diameter of approximately 20mm. Confirmed via https://www.electronics-notes.com/articles/electronic_components/connectors/xlr-connector.php and https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/XLR_connector
40mm x 40mm is a reasonable fan cutout size for 1U rack panel designs. No specific standard found, but commonly used in equipment manufacturing.
0.5 mm tolerance is a reasonable mechanical tolerance specification for CAD drawings. This is consistent with manufacturing standards for sheet metal and machined parts.

privileged_info_summary: This is a synthetic CAD task with no external data. The task specifications are fully compliant with EIA-310 and IEC 60297 standards for 19-inch rack equipment. Panel width (482.6 mm) and height (43.7 mm) match the official 19-inch 1U rack panel standards exactly. The mounting hole spacing (465.1 mm) matches the EIA-310 standard specification of 18 5/16 inches. The connector diameter (20 mm) matches standard XLR audio/control connectors used in rack equipment. The hole diameter (6.0 mm) is a valid metric M6 standard alternative to the more common 10-32 UNC threads. All critical dimensions are verified to be standards-compliant.
